item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, think it would make a great Tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So should this page get an update or should we restart the doomsday clock? Because as far as I know, the only thing that has happened in 2020 as of now is the Angry Video Game Nerd fulfilling his joke of reviewing Raid 2020. Unless that's what the SCP-2217.

+ You know what?
- How about this

Somebody try and make that a tale. The Event is just Mehkane being rebuilt just to watch the Nerds review of Raid 2020. Actually now that I think of it if it actually could swear, then 2399 would make a great Super mecha death christ.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, here we go

item.title: Great SCP article
item.content:encoded: It is time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So am I. :-D

item.title: And then...
item.content:encoded: The fight with FLESH begins. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: welp, i look forward to tomorrow.

I don't have much to say that I've not already said in my other milestone posts, except for this:

The Broken Year is here. 2217 is (hopefully) going to get a complete storyline before the end of the year.

item.title: Re: Welp (also +1)
item.content:encoded:
Do not fear… there is a proposal of 001 showing almost completely assembled broken god. dating almost two centuries back
I would be more concerned about getting his weapon back from the Jupiter.

Edit: Of course this is +1. I don't know how i managed not to upvote this the first time I read it. I look forward the emergency of flesh.

item.title: Welp (also +1)
item.content:encoded:
We are fucked considering the SCP-610 breach occurs this year.
I would love if you would post something on that date. This is one of the best CotBG skips on the site.

item.title: "And yea", MEKHANE decreed, "In Celebration of the Upcoming End, There Shall Be An Update!"
item.content:encoded:
So, a few things.

1) Despite my misgivings, I've changed the theme of this to nuSCP.

2) I've added an image! Source is here: https://pixabay.com/en/beach-milos-greece-greek-island-913560/

3) The Broken Year begins soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this a few years back before joining the site, and it's stayed with me. The world building, linking one of the most popular GOIs and 610, the religious elements, the narrative progression - for me, it all works perfectly.

If I ever write an article half as good as this one, I'll be darned pleased with myself. +1.

I. Have no words. I am officially in the +300 club. If it weren't 2:00 AM here, I'd be jumping for joy.

It's just a number, but it's a big number, and it tells me that I've made an impact on this site. Thank you. All of you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its complicated: the idea seems to be that the physical form would be a permanent conduit/vessel, whereas this was more like a temporary "safemode boot" summoning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor quibble: I'm a little confused about having a ritual to summon Mekhane. I thought the whole issue with the Broken Church was that they needed to physically rebuild their god.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: The second crosslink feels rather forced, since nowhere in the article does it actually talk about about the scenario, nor any Sarkic end times and the like.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This page is already throughly crosslinked. However, I think there are some room for links, specifically to Maxwellism stuff, Sarkic stuff, and Person of Interest File #2273/01.

Editing this:

Broken Church adherents were reportedly accompanied by members of GOI-004B (Cogwork Orthodox) and GOI-004C (Church of Maxwellism), despite reports of intense animosity between these groups.

To this:

Broken Church adherents were reportedly accompanied by members of GOI-004B (Cogwork Orthodox) and GOI-004C (Church of Maxwellism), despite reports of intense animosity between these groups.

Editing this:

SCP-2217 has been reclassified as a Thaumiel-class anomaly, and will be used to prevent Scenario XK-610-Ω.

To this:

SCP-2217 has been reclassified as a Thaumiel-class anomaly, and will be used to prevent Scenario XK-610-Ω

Editing this:

During the prevention of this scenario, all secrecy protocols will be rescinded, with a high probability of leading to an LV-Zero "Lifted Veil" scenario; this has been deemed a necessary risk.

To this:

During the prevention of this scenario, all secrecy protocols will be rescinded, with a high probability of leading to an LV-Zero "Lifted Veil" scenario; this has been deemed a necessary risk.

item.title: Emergency: 2019
item.content:encoded: We need to continue writing something cause 2019 is in one and a half year.

item.title: +250 and some thoughts on it.
item.content:encoded:
In the space of three years, one month and twenty-seven days, 2217 has reached +200. Maybe this will be my first to reach +300? Who knows.

Now, some proper author commentary:

Boring Stuff!
Make the boring stuff go away!

I was in a bad place when I wrote this. I was stressed out from college, both moving to it and attending it. I had to adapt to driving 120 miles round-trip every other weekend to visit home, had to deal with living by myself for the first time. The worst part is, I barely remember any of it, except for strong emotions— rages, depressions, euphorias. Beyond that, it was a haze of stress, and my mind was constantly clouded.

This compounded issues I was having on the wiki; I'd deleted an entire tale series after getting some particularly heartbreaking criticism about it that made me doubt the quality of the whole thing. The GOI Contest the past summer1 had me as captain of my team, and I felt like I failed in that regard. My writing overall suffered, and I produced some of my weakest stuff during this period.2

I felt the need to leave the wiki, if only temporarily, because everything was kind of falling apart for me here, and I needed to focus on schoolwork. I had two ideas for skips I was working on at the time: one was a guy who ate everything he saw3, and the other was just called "Paley's Beach".

"Paley's Beach" was based off of the Watchmaker Analogy, a philosophical argument for intelligent design that came about as a result of the scientific revolution thinking that the universe was too organized to have sprung into being spontaneously, and must have been made by a divine watchmaker. William Paley was one of the big guys behind this theory, and the way I learned the analogy was that a watch found in the middle of nowhere could have been placed there by a person, or by a divine being. In case it isn't clear, I suck at philosophy.

This is the last draft of Paley's Beach, in it's entirety, taken from my sandbox's history:

Item #: SCP-2XXX

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-2XXX is to be closed off to the public, with Standard Cover Story 419 (Beach Cleanup) being enacted to prevent trespassing. A meteorology center has been established near SCP-2XXX to effectively predict lightning strikes in the area,

Description: SCP-2XXX is a 1 km stretch of beach located on an island near [REDACTED], Maryland. The sand of SCP-2XXX is composed of non-anomalous silicates and calcium compounds, as well as several cationic metallic particles that act as a catalyst for the anomaly related to SCP-2XXX. SCP-2XXX also houses a small silicate cliff at the northernmost end, which is partially affected by the anomaly.

Natural processes that take place in SCP-2XXX, including the decay of sea life that washes up on it, erosion of the shoreline, lightning strikes (made more frequent due to the aforementioned cation content), and [FOURTH THING HERE] result in the creation of structures and devices that are seemingly artificial in nature, collectively designated SCP-2XXX-A. Notable SCP-2XXX-A instances include fulgurites that act as electrical lightsources, a grotto within the aforementioned cliffs that produces a large amount of

And that's all I wrote. By September 29th, I was ready to just give up and ask for a temporary revocation of membership so I could focus on schoolwork.

Then, something clicked.

Back during the GOI contest, FortuneFavorsBold, Von Pincier and I all had different roles in building our version of the Church of the Broken God, which was divided roughly as such; FFB was in charge of the Broken Church, Von Pincier was in charge of writing the Cogwork Orthodoxy4 and I did Maxwellism. I made up a good part of Maxwellist lore, and I suck at writing Maxwellism. Cyberpunk just isn't my jam, and from what I can remember, a big part of the reason why Maxwellism even existed was SCP-2360. So thank Silberescher for that.

Like I said above, I felt like a failure to my team during the contest, when we thought all this up. But about a dozen of the ideas we made up for the contest — including SCP-610 being the CotBG's equivalent of Satan, the names MEKHANE and WAN, the Greek origin of the Church5 — and threw them into a metaphorical blender.

In this, I also threw the idea of the Foundation, Global Occult Coalition and Horizon Imitative all working together to stop the Church— both the GOC Hub and the Et Tam Deum Petivi canon page essentially list members of the church as "Shoot on sight" to this day. The three big forces n the 'verse — The Foundation's scientific knowledge and attitude, the GOC'S Magic and Magitech, and the HI's religious fervor — all came together to fight a common enemy. The name "Triumvirate" just came about because I thought it was a cool name that they could all agree on.

On top of that, I added a big dollop of wide-scale conflict with SCP-610, since that was agreed upon as the Church's ultimate evil, with reluctant cooperation thrown in at the end, and a Thaumiel-class. In all, I wrote this in a day and a half, which is the quickest time I've written an SCP from start to finish, not counting the Paley's Beach draft that languished in my sandbox for months.

This is my favorite SCP that I've written, and I'm absolutely ecstatic that it also seems to be the most influential. As of right now,6 the broken-god tag has 119 entries filed under it, which makes it the second-most tagged GOI on the wiki, after marshal-carter-and-dark, which has 147 entries. I'm glad that people read this, as well as the work that Rapid Nomenclature Shift did during the GOI contest, and latched onto it so well. Like, I am legitimately humbled. also, in case you're wondering, dr-wondertainment is in third, with 116 tagged pages.

I didn't do this alone; this was the result of a lot of feedback, patience, and encouragement. I remember giggling like a fool when I posted this, because people liked it. I have to give special thanks to Pincier, Fortune, and the original leader of Rapid Nomenclature Shift, the one who brought us together, Devereaux. Wherever you all are, I'm raising a glass of cider to you tonight.

Footnotes
1. Sidenote: I was a real shit during this contest, that much I do remember. It's a miracle that I wasn't voted off the team. Sorry, guys!
2. Barring 1774, that is; even I forget I wrote that skip.
3. As in, everything he looked at started being digested. Like Cyclops, except worse. Somehow.
4. Which, why isn't there more CogOx stuff out there? It's really good!
5. This was part of my headcanon for a while, since I figured that they would blend right in with a Cult of Hephaestus, worshiping technology and whatnot. Artistic License-- history at its finest.
6. Based on my tinkering about with Jarvis over the course of an hour or so

Seriously. This feels great. Thank you, everyone, who's voted and commented on this, everyone who's written about it, drawn it, recorded readings of it. You guys rock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Logged in to upvote.
Extremely enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unknown Allied Entity

I think i saw it somewhere, but can't remember where.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded:
Only great things reach +200, Ihp.

And this is a great thing. :)

item.title: Re: +199
item.content:encoded:
NO LONGER

just kidding re-voting now

don't kill me

Holy SHIITAKE MUSHROOMS.

When did this hit +200?! Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Several of the buildings appeared to be in the process of being consumed by a substance resembling pahoehoe lava. From this mass, several beings resembling individuals infected with SCP-610

flesh lava

jesus fucking christ

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is Church of the Broken God done right! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like others have said, I love former foes teaming up against a common foe. Especially when this is a battle between a un division, scientific organization, yawheh worshippers and clock worshippers vs. extremely hostile meat.

You have earned my +1, and deserve many more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation, Horizon Initiative and Global Occult Coalition assets (Codenamed "Triumvirate") are currently collaborating with Broken God members to attempt to summon UAE-Aleph-15 as a further countermeasure to the aforementioned XK scenario.

The Triumvirate, a joint task force with the Horizon Initiative and Global Occult Coalition, has found that these symbols correspond to a large-scale summoning ritual used to revive UAE-Aleph-015, or "The Broken God/MEKHANE/WAN".

I think I know what the "AE" in "UAE" stands for (Anomalous Entity), but what does the "U" stand for? Unknown? Unclassed? Unidentified? Something else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off: +1. any quibbles do not change the strength of this piece. I'm very fond of seeing major plots slide through SCP reports as well as tales. Love the execution.

But…I gotta agree with Canary upthread. I think it would make it stronger (and not force you to remember to update it in four years) to blackbox the year of the future posts. By putting anything past date of publication redacted, the reader is left to wonder if this happened last week, or years down the road.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've never been able to understand this one.

The concept is OTT that I'm surprised it works. And yet it does. Great stuff.

Looks like I'm the 100th upvote. Congrats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like how the CotBG has been changing from a group of nutjobs who worship gears to an active religious group who also happens to worship machines. Also, yay for GoI intercooperation!
All of the +1s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome codename. Hammer and Anvil!

breaking stereotype is almost always interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a minor nitpick, but I thought it was generally agreed that self-replicating objects cause NK class scenarios, not XKs. So the first sentence of the " current" containment procedures should say "SCP-2217 is currently undergoing weaponization in order to prevent Scenario NK-610-Ω from occurring," instead of what it currently says. However, if the author doesn't change it, I'll chalk it up to no canon and my upvote will stay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-610 has been used as an antagonist figure for the CotBG a few times before but usually in tales told from outside the Foundation's perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of some old CotBG history post mentioning an ancient war between The Broken God and The God of Flesh. Anyone got a source for that? Oh, and +1 of course, I'm a sucker for the Church

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is excellent.
+10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god.

Plot advancement.

This is going to be amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Church of the Broken God vs. The Flesh That Hates ROUND ONE FIGHT

There's nothing I don't love about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An enthusiastic +1 from me. I really enjoyed reading about the various factions' back-and-forth and their reluctant-on-all-sides working together (gotta love a team-up). I'm not sure if I would have liked it better or worse if the most recent entry/entries were taking place in the present (like that one "active breach" SCP) instead of a few years from now (or if all of the years were just blacked out to leave it ambiguous), but it didn't bug. This is a model case of cross-referencing done right, and an engaging read. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Escaped chemical engineering major to the rescue:

metal+salt solution= battery (and corrosion)

metal deposits + ocean + eldritch monolith = mother of all spark plugs.

As long as it has the sort of massive surface deposits you can find in the metal-rich Mediterranean volcanic islands, its got all the iron, zinc, lead, copper, and electricity it could ever need. You wouldn't get shocked aside from lightning strikes because the island itself would be the capacitor. You're grounded relative to the island, but the island is going to be attracting lightning and generating St Elmo's Fire every time a decent cloud rolls by.

Bonus: Sand struck by lightning forms glass. Glass is an insulator. Metals encased in or composited with glass insulators in various ways are also known as transistors, capacitors, vacuum/gas tubes, and industrial grade conduits. A sacred site for the CotBG indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone is trying to wrest me from my slumber…

And they're doing a pretty good job. I really like the direction you've taken this, and even though it doesn't quite fit the timeline I had in mind re: 20 GOTO 10, it's still good enough that I'm feeling pretty inspired to get back on the horse after… After.

+1. Good show. Could use a tonal scrub around the scripture and some of the incident reporting, but I ain't shook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. 610 as the CotBG's ultimate evil makes so much sense I'm amazed I never thought of it before. And the formation of the Triumvirate, the Foundation/GOC/Horizon Initiative allying with the Church, actively preparing for very likely lifting of the veil - love this all so damn much. My headcanon puts this into the Et Tam Deum Petivi story arc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I've said before, this isn't the kind of story I would upvote. However, the story here is compelling and interesting in its implications. It's well structured and the pacing is straightforward/easy to follow.

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this so damn much. Like, nothing makes me happier in fiction than to see morally good or neutral enemies become allies in order to fight objective evil. And nothing has influenced my headcanon this much since I read the Between Two Trees series; before, I'd have never connected the Broken God to SCP-610. I just figured the Foundation had nuked it but the document was outdated. That theory is gone, and I've never been happier to see something I came up with go. This is my new favorite skip.

Edit: Was Kythera chosen as the location for this because of the Antikythera Mechanism, but with Kythera instead so as to be less obvious?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this in chat and loved it. This is a fantastic work of scip and frankly, cross-referencing done right. So many props to you.

Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could get behind that. 'Twould be fun to write. But I've got too many half-baked ideas and half-finished things floating in my sandbox for another, I think…

/sigh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And upvoted.
Heh, it's five years since I took any chemistry, btw.

item.title: Snip
item.content:encoded: Of course it shows up the instant I post the second one. Bloody wikidot.

item.title: Special Thanks Post
item.content:encoded:
I felt this deserved its own post, since I feel like a jackass for not mentioning it in my author commentary post.

This SCP couldn't have happened if not for one of the most awesome people on the wiki, FortuneFavorsBold. The inspiration for this idea comes from the GOI contest, and we were on the Church of the Broken God team together. When we were spitballing ideas, he thought it would be cool if we made 610 Satan to the Broken God. And so, we did, but we never got a chance to properly writing about it outside of a few oblique allusions. This was my attempt to make these ideas whole.

So, Fortune, if you're reading this, you made this happen. Thanks, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

I knew there was a reason I only got a B in my last chem class. Gimme a sec.

E: hopefully that rectifies it somewhat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, that sounds possible, but I mean, the term is bugging me more than anything. I haven't met it anywhere else, and uh, given cation is a + charged ion, it suggests there are chunks of ionized metal lying on the beach, without having the charge imbalance dissipate into the enviroment.

(In the case I'm derping around massively, which actually might be the case, if you can pull up a definition from somewhere that'd dissipate the issue)

Which actually could be a really neat anomaly if it was given attention (basically, static electricity reservoir that'd jolt you badly when you pick it up, but it lies on the beach without discharging. Oh, and discharge one of them , toss it back, and watch the world's littlest lightning show as the charges between it and its neighbours balance x3), but like, not enough lampshade for it, which makes it sound like bad science rather than an anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me want to see 610 explored more. The church scene in the original SCP article suggested retained sapience and behavior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still unsure about the 610 inclusion, or the cationic particles on the beach, though I like the rest.

Can't help you on the former part, but on the latter… believe it or not, there are some rocks that are capable of attracting lightning. Granite is a known lightning magnet, and any part of soil with enough metallic and/or magnetic content can attract electrical discharges.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I enjoy about the piece is how well suited 610 and the CoTBG are as mortal enemies. It makes too much sense: a group dedicated to the replacement of flesh fighting against a corruption that is nothing but flesh. I'd love to see some tales about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still unsure about the 610 inclusion, or the cationic particles on the beach, though I like the rest.
Neutral vote, ultimately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw yeah. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally really like the whole idea of this SCP. It has a well-thought out backstory and it has a great example of good crosslinking. That being said, my only qualm is about the future date. While there have been quite a few hinting and shady dates in a lot of SCPs, I feel like setting documents in the future kind of detracts from the mystery. Regardless, I'll give it +1 for being interesting and well thought out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really digging the notion that 610 isn't just a visceral disease and is in fact something with far greater importance.

item.title: Re: "And So," decreed the Broken One, "There will be Author Commentary!"
item.content:encoded: Of course Kaktus gets love! You all get love! Love for everybody!
